([English](/wiki/English_language "English language"): **Mary's Square**, i.e., St. Mary, [Our Lady](/wiki/Blessed_Virgin_Mary "Blessed Virgin Mary")'s Square) is a [central square](/wiki/Town_square "Town square") in the city centre of [Munich](/wiki/Munich "Munich"), Germany. It has been the city's main square since 1158.
For more and detailed pictures of the column see the respective [German-language article](https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mariens%C3%A4ule_(M%C3%BCnchen) "de:Mariensäule (München)") on wikipedia.de.

Marienplatz

**Marienplatz** ([English](/wiki/English_language "English language"): **Mary's Square**, i.e., St. Mary, [Our Lady](/wiki/Blessed_Virgin_Mary "Blessed Virgin Mary")'s Square) is a [central square](/wiki/Town_square "Town square") in the city centre of [Munich](/wiki/Munich "Munich"), Germany. It has been the city's main square since 1158.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Marienplatz&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

During the [Middle Ages](/wiki/Middle_Ages "Middle Ages"), markets and tournaments were held in the Marienplatz, which was originally called *Markth* ("market"), *Schranne* ("[grain market](/wiki/Grain_trade "Grain trade")") and later *Schrannenplatz* ("grain market square"). After the grain market was moved into the modern glass-and-iron [Schranne](https://infopediadiary.com/travel-guides-marienplatz-munich-guide/) near "Blumenstrasse" in 1853, the square received its new name, starting 9 October 1854.

* [![View of the New Town Hall and Frauenkirche looking westward, 1656](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f7/Der_Marckt_zu_M%C3%BCnchen_1656_%28Marienplatz%29_-_Matth%C3%A4us_Merian_d.J.jpg/120px-Der_Marckt_zu_M%C3%BCnchen_1656_%28Marienplatz%29_-_Matth%C3%A4us_Merian_d.J.jpg)](/wiki/File:Der_Marckt_zu_M%C3%BCnchen_1656_(Marienplatz)_-_Matth%C3%A4us_Merian_d.J.jpg "View of the New Town Hall and Frauenkirche looking westward, 1656")

  View of the [New Town Hall](/wiki/New_Town_Hall_(Munich) "New Town Hall (Munich)") and [Frauenkirche](/wiki/Frauenkirche,_Munich "Frauenkirche, Munich") looking westward, 1656
* [![View of the square towards the west from an 1838 print](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e7/M%C3%BCnchen_Schrannenplatz_Soeltl_1838.jpg/120px-M%C3%BCnchen_Schrannenplatz_Soeltl_1838.jpg)](/wiki/File:M%C3%BCnchen_Schrannenplatz_Soeltl_1838.jpg "View of the square towards the west from an 1838 print")

  View of the square towards the west from an 1838 print
* [![View of the square towards the east from an 1853 painting](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d3/Anton_Doll_Der_Marienplatz_in_M%C3%BCnchen_1853.jpg/120px-Anton_Doll_Der_Marienplatz_in_M%C3%BCnchen_1853.jpg)](/wiki/File:Anton_Doll_Der_Marienplatz_in_M%C3%BCnchen_1853.jpg "View of the square towards the east from an 1853 painting")

  View of the square towards the east from an 1853 painting

## Architecture

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Marienplatz&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Architecture")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b9/Neues_Rathaus_in_M%C3%BCnchen_-_Panorama.jpg/250px-Neues_Rathaus_in_M%C3%BCnchen_-_Panorama.jpg)](/wiki/File:Neues_Rathaus_in_M%C3%BCnchen_-_Panorama.jpg)

[New Town Hall](/wiki/New_Town_Hall_(Munich) "New Town Hall (Munich)")

Marienplatz was named after the [Mariensäule](/wiki/Mariens%C3%A4ule "Mariensäule"), a [Marian column](/wiki/Marian_and_Holy_Trinity_columns "Marian and Holy Trinity columns") erected in its centre in 1638 to celebrate the end of Swedish occupation. Today the Marienplatz is dominated by the [New Town Hall](/wiki/New_Town_Hall_(Munich) "New Town Hall (Munich)") (Neues Rathaus) on the north side, and the [Old Town Hall](/wiki/Old_Town_Hall,_Munich "Old Town Hall, Munich") (Altes Rathaus, a reconstructed [Gothic](/wiki/Gothic_architecture "Gothic architecture") council hall with a ballroom and tower) on the east side.

The [Glockenspiel](/wiki/Rathaus-Glockenspiel "Rathaus-Glockenspiel") in the tower of the New Town Hall was inspired by the tournaments that were held in the square during the Middle Ages, and draws millions of tourists a year. Furthermore, the pedestrian zone between [Karlsplatz](/wiki/Karlsplatz_(Stachus) "Karlsplatz (Stachus)") and Marienplatz is a crowded area with numerous shops and restaurants.

## Mary's Column

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Marienplatz&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Mary's Column")]

Main article: [Mariensäule](/wiki/Mariens%C3%A4ule "Mariensäule")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/36/Marienstatue_-_panoramio_%281%29.jpg/250px-Marienstatue_-_panoramio_%281%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Marienstatue_-_panoramio_(1).jpg)

Marienplatz with [Mariensäule](/wiki/Mariens%C3%A4ule "Mariensäule")

The *Mariensäule* is a [Marian column](/wiki/Marian_and_Holy_Trinity_columns "Marian and Holy Trinity columns") located on the Marienplatz in [Munich](https://infopediadiary.com/travel-guides-marienplatz-munich-guide/), Germany. Mary is revered here as *[Patrona Bavariae](https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Patrona_Bavariae "de:Patrona Bavariae")* (Latin: Protector of [Bavaria](/wiki/Bavaria "Bavaria")).
It was erected in 1638 to celebrate the end of [Swedish](/wiki/Sweden "Sweden") occupation during the [Thirty Years' War](/wiki/Thirty_Years%27_War "Thirty Years' War"), following a respective vow by [Duke Elector Maximilian I of Bavaria](/wiki/Maximilian_I,_Elector_of_Bavaria "Maximilian I, Elector of Bavaria") if the ducal residential cities of [Munich](/wiki/Munich "Munich") and [Landshut](/wiki/Landshut "Landshut") were spared from war destruction. The column is topped by a [golden](/wiki/Gold "Gold") [statue](/wiki/Statue "Statue") of the [Virgin Mary](/wiki/Mary_(mother_of_Jesus) "Mary (mother of Jesus)") standing on a crescent moon as the [Queen of Heaven](/wiki/Queen_of_Heaven "Queen of Heaven"), created in 1590. The figure was originally located in the [Frauenkirche](/wiki/Munich_Frauenkirche "Munich Frauenkirche"). *Mariensäule* in Munich was the first column of this type built north of the [Alps](/wiki/Alps "Alps") and inspired the construction of other Marian columns in this part of Europe.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

At each corner of the column's pedestal is a statue of a [putto](/wiki/Putto "Putto"), created by [Ferdinand Murmann](https://infopediadiary.com/travel-guides-marienplatz-munich-guide/). The four putti are each depicted fighting a different beast, symbolizing the city's overcoming of adversities: war represented by the lion, pestilence by the [cockatrice](/wiki/Cockatrice "Cockatrice"), hunger or famine by the dragon and [heresy](/wiki/Heresy "Heresy") by the serpent.

## Christmas Market

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Marienplatz&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Christmas Market")]

Three weeks before Christmas the *[Christkindlmarkt](/wiki/Christkindlmarkt_at_Marienplatz "Christkindlmarkt at Marienplatz")* opens at Marienplatz and other squares in the city, selling Christmas goods and food and drinks.

## Transport

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Marienplatz&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Transport")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/62/M%C3%BCnchen_-_Marienplatz_%28Luftbild%29.jpg/250px-M%C3%BCnchen_-_Marienplatz_%28Luftbild%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:M%C3%BCnchen_-_Marienplatz_(Luftbild).jpg)

Aerial view of Marienplatz

The square is served by the [Munich Marienplatz station](/wiki/Munich_Marienplatz_station "Munich Marienplatz station"), offering both [U-Bahn](/wiki/Munich_U-Bahn "Munich U-Bahn") and [S-Bahn](/wiki/Munich_S-Bahn "Munich S-Bahn") access.

A new station, [Marienhof](https://infopediadiary.com/travel-guides-marienplatz-munich-guide/), will be built to the north of the square, as part of the second S-Bahn tunnel project (Zweite Stammstrecke). The new station will also connect to the existing lines.[[2]](#cite_note-2)
